meaning: adj.拥有土地的,有关土地所有权的n.作为地主
examples: Hadrian belonged to an upwardly mobile Spanish landowning elite that had grown rich selling olive oil.
Greek philosophers belonged to, or were employed by, the landowning class; they therefore disapproved of interest.
He was born into a landowning family in what is now Tamil Nadu, in southern India.
Similarly the Akali Dal, in Punjab, which long relied on prosperous and landowning jats, reached out to dalits.
They did so in mid-2005, when shares of landowning companies reached their zenith.
